data.title: Foreign investments in German businesses doubled to EUR 96 billion in 2025 from EUR 43 billion in 2024, per IW study.
data.description: According to a recent report from the German Economic Institute (IW), foreign companies have invested a total of EUR 96 billion into their operations in Germany. This marks a significant increase from the EUR 43 billion invested in the previous year, as revealed in the study. The data, sourced from Germany's national bank, the Deutsche Bundesbank, highlights the growing confidence and interest from foreign businesses in the German market. The report, published on Jan. 26, 2026, in Berlin, signifies the strong economic relationship between international companies and the German business landscape.

data.title: Bain & Co. forecasts slower retail sales growth in US, UK, France, Germany in 2026. US sales to reach $5.3 trillion, up by 3.5% from 2025.
data.description: Bain forecasts that retail sales in the United States are expected to increase by 3.5% year over year, reaching $5.3 trillion in 2026. This growth rate is slightly lower than the estimated 4.0% growth experienced in 2025. The UK is projected to see a 2% growth in retail sales, with minimal volume increases in the food sector and a slight decline in non-food categories. Meanwhile, retail sales in France are anticipated to deliver...

data.title: Experts foresee HBAR hitting $0.16 by month-end from its current price at $0.11, despite market challenges.
data.description: The cryptocurrency Hedera (HBAR) is currently trading at $0.11. Analysts are forecasting a potential 45% increase, up to $0.16, by the end of the month. However, there are some obstacles in the near term due to bearish momentum and a neutral Relative Strength Index (RSI).

data.title: INOVAIT in Toronto is calling for Winter 2026 Pilot Fund applications, offering up to 150,000 CAD for image-guided therapy and AI projects.
data.description: INOVAIT, a leading image-guided therapy and artificial intelligence network in Canada, has launched a call for applications for the Winter 2026 Pilot Fund. This fund, a non-dilutive funding initiative, aims to back innovative research and development projects in image-guided therapy (IGT) and artificial intelligence (AI) technologies. The current Pilot Fund round has earmarked up to 150,000 CAD to progress promising technologies through 9- to 12-month R&D projects. This initiative presents a significant opportunity for researchers and innovators in the field to access financial support for their advancements.

data.title: A2RL Drone Championship in Abu Dhabi showcases AI in autonomous flight.
data.description: The A2RL Drone Championship held in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, has emerged as a trailblazer in integrating artificial intelligence into autonomous flight technology. This event showcased cutting-edge advancements in drone technology, emphasizing AI's role in enhancing unmanned aerial vehicle capabilities. The championship attracted a significant number of participants and spectators, underscoring the growing interest in AI-driven innovations in the field of autonomous flight.

data.title: First Citizens BancShares beats Q4 estimates, earns $51.27/share and $2.435 billion in sales. Analysts revise forecasts downwards. CEO happy with performance.
data.description: First Citizens BancShares Inc (NASDAQ: FCNCA) announced positive fourth-quarter earnings on Friday. The company reported earnings per share of $51.27, surpassing the analyst consensus of $43.91 per share. Quarterly sales stood at $2.435 billion, also exceeding the analyst estimate of $2.289 billion. Frank B. Holding, Jr., the Chairman and CEO, expressed satisfaction over the results stating, "We achieved strong loan growth, particularly in Global Fund Banking, and maintained stable credit quality. Additionally, we returned $900 million to shareholders through share repurchases and prepaid $2.5 billion of the Purchase Money Note during the quarter."

data.title: Horizon Europe separates from First Brands Group, unaffected by U.S. operations wind down involving Brake Parts Inc., Cardone, Autolite.
data.description: A company in Germany known as Horizon Europe, a prominent manufacturer of branded towing and trailering equipment globally, recently issued a statement in response to the announcement made by First Brands Group, LLC. First Brands declared the systematic closure of some of its North American operations, such as Brake Parts Inc., Cardone, and Autolite. However, Horizon Europe confirmed that it is currently in the process of disassociating from First Brands and asserted that its operations remain unaffected by the ongoing business adjustments within the U.S. The specifics of the impact on Frist Brands were not mentioned, but Horizon Europe reassured its continuity amidst these changes.

data.title: WIF price set to recover to $0.42-$0.43 by February after technical consolidation. Short-term target at $0.35-$0.36.
data.description: Summary of WIF Price Prediction: In the short term (1 week), the target price range is between $0.35 to $0.36. Looking ahead to the medium term (1 month), the forecast indicates a range of $0.42 to $0.43. A bullish breakout is expected at $0.36, while the critical support level is yet to be determined.

data.title: CBL Properties shares 2025 tax reporting info; Ordinary Dividends: $0.50 per share, Capital Gain Distribution: TBD.
data.description: CBL Properties revealed tax information for the 2025 distributions on its Common stock (NYSE:CBL). Each share received distributions in two categories: Ordinary Dividends and Capital Gain Distribution. The Record Date and Payable Date for these distributions were provided for shareholders' reference. The company allocated the 2025 Ordinary Dividends to Box 1a on Form 1099-DIV, while the 2025 Capital Gain Distribution was detailed separately.This was specific to CBL & Associates Properties, Inc., Common Stock with CUSIP number 124830-878 and listed on NYSE under the symbol CBL. Shareholders were advised to refer to this information for accurate tax reporting purposes.

data.title: Crypto funds saw $1.73B in outflows last week, largest since November, as Bitcoin, Ethereum ETFs bleed. U.S.-led redemptions intensified.
data.description: Investors pulled out $1.73 billion from digital asset investment products last week as redemptions led by the United States increased.

data.title: SLB's Q4 revenue hits $9.745B, up 5% from the year prior, exceeding analyst expectations. EPS at 78 cents; outlook optimistic.
data.description: SLB Limited (NYSE: SLB) posted its fourth-quarter results, indicating a sequential improvement as global upstream activity stabilized. The company reported fourth-quarter revenue of $9.745 billion, a 5% increase from the previous year and surpassing the analyst estimate of $9.547 billion. Diluted GAAP earnings per share were 55 cents, down from 77 cents year over year. Additionally, diluted EPS, excluding charges and credits, stood at 78 cents, a decline from 92 cents in the previous year but beating the 74-cent estimate. The CEO mentioned the challenges faced in 2025 due to lower commodity prices, geopolitical uncertainty, and an oversupplied oil market. To address these challenges, the company focused on enhancing resilience by accelerating strategies such as emphasizing production and recovery, increasing the deployment of AI solutions, and rapidly expanding the Data Center Solutions business. For further details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: First Brands Group starts wind down of Brake Parts Inc., Cardone, and Autolite units in North America. Assessing transition options.
data.description: A prominent automotive parts supplier, First Brands Group, LLC, based in Rochester Hills, Michigan, has recently disclosed the start of winding down specific business operations in North America. This includes well-known brands like Brake Parts Inc., Cardone, and Autolite. The company is actively working to transition these units to new ownership while its other business segments remain operational. First Brands Group, LLC is focusing on evaluating various options for the future following a thorough assessment in recent months.

data.title: PEPE price gains 3.33% daily despite mixed signals. Neutral RSI at 44.09 with resistance levels looming.
data.description: PEPE experienced a daily gain of 3.33%, however, it is currently facing bearish MACD signals. Technical analysis indicates a neutral RSI of 44.09. The meme coin is approaching key resistance levels in its trend.

data.title: Traders predict gradual rise of XRP to $10 after stabilizing around $1.80-$2 support, expecting sideways movement.
data.description: XRP is currently maintaining strong support levels between $1.80 and $2. Several indicators are signaling a potential period of sideways price movement before a significant increase, possibly reaching double digits.

data.title: Gray showcasing design-build expertise at the 2026 IPPE in Atlanta, will engage with customers and partners in the industry.
data.description: In anticipation of the 2026 International Production & Processing Expo (IPPE) in Atlanta, the global protein and food processing industry is gearing up to convene for the event. Gray is eager to engage with customers, partners, and key figures in the industry during the expo. The expo will take place in Atlanta, GA, from January 26 to January 28, 2026.

data.title: InMode's stock soars on rumored $1.1B takeover bid by foreign fund, hitting three-month high. via Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: An analyst suggested that a foreign investment fund may acquire the medical aesthetics outlet InMode for a staggering $1.1 billion. This speculation caused a surge in InMode's stock on Monday, propelling it to a three-month high. This potential takeover offer has attracted significant attention and interest in the market.

data.title: Wall Street analysts divided on Apple stock reaction to holiday-quarter results. Report coming Thursday.
data.description: Market analysts on Wall Street have varying opinions regarding the potential response of Apple's stock following the announcement of its holiday-quarter results this Thursday. The sentiment surrounding Apple's stock is mixed as investors await the report.
